About Marcus

One of Britain's most distinctive sporting voices

Marcus Watson is one of only a handful of British athletes to have stood on an Olympic podium in rugby. As part of the historic Team GB Rugby Sevens squad at Rio 2016, the first time the sport had featured at the Olympics since 1924. He helped the team defeat South Africa in the semi-final and reach the final against Fiji, returning home with a silver medal.

What makes Marcus's story distinctive isn't just the medal. While representing England and then Great Britain on the world sevens circuit, Marcus completed his university degree, graduating with a first-class honours in Business and Management Studies. He knows first-hand what it takes to hold two major ambitions at once and excel at both.

Now retired from professional rugby, Marcus speaks with warmth, authenticity and practical insight, not from a script but from lived experience at the sharpest end of elite sport.

Speaking Topics

Three talks. One story. Countless applications.

01

The Olympic Journey

From turning up as one of 27 athletes representing England, Scotland and Wales, to standing on the podium at Rio. Marcus shares the inside story of what it truly takes to compete for a place on the world's greatest stage.

  • Resilience and coping with setbacks
  • Goal-setting and discipline at the highest level
  • Performance under pressure
  • What the Olympic Games feels like from the inside
  • Turning perceived weaknesses into strengths
02

Teamwork Under Pressure

27 players from three nations. One squad. Twelve spots. The selection process for Rio was as brutal as any in British sport, yet the team came together brilliantly and won silver.

  • Building culture when individuals compete for places
  • Leadership, communication and trust under pressure
  • What 'team first' looks like when stakes are highest
  • Lessons from the GB selection process
03

Sport Meets Education

Marcus is living proof that sport and academic excellence don't have to be a trade-off. Graduating with a first-class degree while competing internationally, he speaks candidly about managing time, identity and dual ambition.

  • Balancing elite sport with academic study
  • Time management, focus and dual identity
  • Why education gave Marcus an edge as an athlete
  • Inspiration for students holding multiple ambitions

Who Marcus Speaks To

Every talk tailored. The story stays real.

🏫

Schools

Inspiring young people to believe in themselves, stay in education, and understand what hard work looks like at the very highest level. Marcus connects naturally with young audiences. His story is relatable, honest and full of moments students can picture themselves in.

🎓

Universities

Particularly powerful for student athletes managing sport alongside study, or any students trying to hold multiple ambitions at once. Marcus's first-class degree, earned during an international rugby career, is a genuinely rare and honest story.

💼

Corporate

Elite sport as a lens for high-performance culture, team dynamics, leadership under pressure and what peak performance really demands day-to-day. The GB selection story (27 players, three nations, only 12 spots) is one of the most striking examples of competitive teamwork in modern British sport.

🎤

Conferences

A keynote with a genuine story at its heart. Equal parts inspiring and practical, leaving audiences with things they can take back to their work. Marcus is comfortable with audiences of any size and adapts his delivery to the format.

"

We were 27 players: English, Scottish and Welsh, all fighting for 12 spots. Learning how to compete against your team-mates every single day, and still come together as one unit, was the most powerful lesson I've ever had in a team environment.

Marcus Watson  ·  Team GB Rugby Sevens, Rio 2016
